# `sesam verify --truncate` walks git history and detects a shortened audit log:
# the log at each commit must be a prefix of the next, never shrink.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'c1'

# Snapshot the c1 log. It lands outside .sesam/sesam.yml so .gitignore keeps it
# untracked and it survives the later commits.
cp .sesam/audit/log.jsonl log-c1.jsonl

# Grow the log with a new secret and commit. History is still consistent.
exec sesam add s.txt --group dev
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'c2'
exec sesam verify --truncate
stderr 'Audit log history: ok'

# Roll the log back to its (shorter) c1 content but move HEAD forward: the
# parent commit's log is now longer than HEAD's -> truncation.
cp log-c1.jsonl .sesam/audit/log.jsonl
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'c3'

# no-disk isolates the history check from the on-disk root-hash mismatch the
# rollback also introduces.
! exec sesam --verify-mode no-disk verify --truncate
stderr 'not a prefix'

-- s.txt --
top secret
